title: Here’s Why Some Humans Can Hear Super Low-Frequency Sounds
author: Jake Currie
text: Subscribe to skip adsAdvertisementNeuroscienceHere’s Why Some Humans Can Hear Super Low-Frequency SoundsIt’s vibesJCBy Jake Currie3:30 PM CDT on July 10, 2026Share on FacebookShare on X (formerly Twitter)Share on RedditShare on EmailShare on BlueskyIf you’ve ever experienced a feeling of unease in a spooky building at night, it might be caused by infrasound. These low frequency sound waves (below 20 hz) can come from furnaces, generators, ventilation systems, and more. While infrasound waves are technically outside of the human range of hearing, there’s plenty of evidence we can perceive them. In fact, previous research has shown that listening to infrasound played with music sends people’s cortisol levels up and makes them feel irritable. Subscribe to skip adsFeatured VideoPowered By 10 Are Aliens Avoiding Earth Because It’s Too Expensive?ShareNextStayIn other words, even though our ears can’t hear it, our brains somehow know it’s there, and new research published in Scientific Reports is shedding light on how.Read more: “The Science of Spooky Sounds”Subscribe to skip adsAdvertisement
